title: Monotone-Constrained Booster For Pit-Window Physics
summary: Several engineered features should have mostly one-direction effects in realistic regimes: larger tyre-age pressure, larger finish-tyre deficit, and larger positive lap-time loss should not usually reduce pit risk.
rationale: Several engineered features should have mostly one-direction effects in realistic regimes: larger tyre-age pressure, larger finish-tyre deficit, and larger positive lap-time loss should not usually reduce pit risk. XGBoost supports monotone constraints specifically for injecting that prior and reducing implausible oscillations.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost companion model with monotone `+1` on features like `tyre_age_vs_expected`, `finish_tyre_deficit`, `lap_delta_loss`, `compound_pit_window_pressure`, and maybe `estimated_laps_remaining` only where sign is defensible. Keep unconstrained features for context and compare against the same feature set without constraints.
expected_effect: Likely a small but robust gain via better extrapolation to unseen drivers/races and less overfit on noisy pit-window edges.
risk: Wrong-sign constraints can cap performance; the constraint set must stay narrow and only cover features with strong physical priors.
